QF.id: VII Introduction to Securities Broking Operations_49

question.text: In the Securities Lending and Borrowing Scheme (SLBS), the second/reverse leg settlement occurs on:

answer.text: The 1st Thursday of the respective series month
The last Friday of the month
Any date chosen by the borrower

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SLBS contracts have monthly contracts up to 12 months with the second/reverse leg settlement on the 1st Thursday of the respective series month. In the reverse leg, the borrower returns securities to the lender. **The Trap:** The first leg (lending) and reverse leg (return) are separate events. Corporate actions and benefits remain with the lender (actual owner) throughout. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers securities lending in the derivatives context. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** SLBS key features: screen-based anonymous matching, lending fee quoted per share, eligible securities = F&O stocks + non-F&O stocks + ETFs, settlement guarantee by clearing corporation with initial margin + daily MTM. Used for short selling, arbitrage, and earning returns on idle holdings.
